methods.method_name: Plant Cultivation and Heat Stress Application
methods.description: Procedure for growing maize plants under controlled glasshouse conditions, applying natural heat stress at the reproductive stage, and managing environmental factors like drought.

steps.description: The seedlings of two inbreds were raised during the second week of March in glasshouse conditions at 28°C/23°C and 16 h light (Day)/8 h dark (Night) photoperiod at the School of Agricultural Biotechnology, Punjab Agricultural University, India, during spring 2016.
steps.searchable_summary: Raise maize seedlings of specified inbred lines (LM 11, CML 25) under controlled glasshouse conditions.

steps.description: The plants were grown till they reached the reproductive stage ( Figure 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Grow plants until they reach the reproductive stage.

steps.description: At the reproductive growth stage, from tassel emergence to early grain-filling (lag-phase), maize plants were exposed to natural heat stress and experienced 42°C during the daytime and 35°C during the nighttime.
steps.searchable_summary: Expose maize plants at the reproductive stage to natural heat stress (42°C day / 35°C night).

steps.description: To maintain the microclimate conditions with low RH (<40%) and to avoid the compound effect of drought and heat stress, regular irrigation was applied for at least two weeks during tassel emergence until one week after pollination, which increases the probability of irreversible damage due to heat stress.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply regular irrigation during the heat stress period to avoid confounding drought stress.

methods.description: Method for collecting specific reproductive tissues (flag leaf, pollen, ovule) from heat-stressed maize plants, ensuring purity, pooling replicates, and preserving samples for downstream molecular analysis.

steps.description: Three different tissue samples, viz. flag leaf, pollen, and ovule from the inbreds, LM 11 and CML 25, were collected in 5 replicates after five days of pollination.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect flag leaf, pollen, and ovule samples from heat-stressed maize inbreds (LM 11, CML 25) at a specific time point (5 days post-pollination) with 5 replicates.
outputs: Raw tissue samples (flag leaf, pollen, ovule) with replicates

steps.description: Tissue purity was maintained by bagging the tassel and cob.
steps.searchable_summary: Ensure tissue purity by bagging tassels and cobs.

steps.description: Ovules were isolated from ear florets with a silk length of~10 cm by removing the silk and ovary wall with forceps and cutting the ovule at its base from the floret under the microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: Isolate ovules from ear florets using dissection tools under a microscope.

steps.description: Each tissue was pooled (pool of five plants) for each inbred to reduce biological sampling error.
steps.searchable_summary: Pool tissue samples from 5 plants for each tissue type and inbred line.
outputs: Pooled tissue samples (per tissue, per inbred)

steps.description: A total of six samples (3 tissues x 2 inbreds) were immediately frozen in liquid nitrogen and stored at -80°C until processed for RNA isolations.
steps.searchable_summary: Immediately freeze pooled samples in liquid nitrogen and store at -80°C.

methods.description: Protocol for extracting total RNA from collected maize tissue samples and assessing its quality and quantity using standard laboratory equipment.

steps.description: Total RNA was isolated from each tissue sample (flag leaf, pollen, and ovule) of LM 11 and CML 25 under heat stress conditions using the NucleoSpin RNA Plant kit (Macherey-Nagel, Duren, Germany) according to the manufacturer's protocol.
steps.searchable_summary: Isolate total RNA from plant tissue samples using a commercial RNA isolation kit.
inputs: Stored tissue samples (flag leaf, pollen, ovule)

steps.description: The RNA integrity number (RIN) and concentration were checked using an Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer (Agilent Technologies, Inc., Santa Clara, CA, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: Check RNA integrity (RIN) and concentration using an Ag Agilent Bioanalyzer.

steps.description: The RNA (1µg) having RIN values >6 was used for further analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Select RNA samples with RIN values greater than 6 for downstream analysis.
inputs: Total RNA samples with RIN values

methods.description: Process for preparing cDNA libraries from qualified total RNA samples and sequencing them using the Illumina platform to generate raw transcriptome data.

steps.description: 5 mg of total RNA was used for mRNA enrichment, and complementary DNA (cDNA) library was constructed using the manufacturer's protocol of Illumina HiSeq 2500 RNA library preparation kit (Illumina, San Diego, CA).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform mRNA enrichment and construct cDNA libraries from total RNA using an Illumina RNA library preparation kit.
inputs: Illumina HiSeq 2500 RNA library preparation kit

steps.description: The quality and quantity of cDNA libraries were checked using the Qubit 2.0 Fluorometer (Thermo Scientific) and Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer (Agilent Technologies, Singapore).
steps.searchable_summary: Check the quality and quantity of cDNA libraries using a Qubit Fluorometer and Agilent Bioanalyzer.

steps.description: The libraries were then sequenced using HiSeq Illumina 2500 sequencing platform (Illumina, San Diego, CA) outsourced from the Nucleome Informatics Pvt. Ltd, Hyderabad, India. High throughput sequencing of transcriptome libraries generated an aggregate of 2.93 billion raw reads accounting for 45.19 Gb of data.
steps.searchable_summary: Sequence cDNA libraries using an Illumina HiSeq platform.

methods.description: Bioinformatic pipeline for processing raw sequencing reads, including quality control, trimming, filtering, and de novo transcriptome assembly.

steps.description: The raw reads were processed with FastQC (Andrews, 2010) to check the quality of the sequences.
steps.searchable_summary: Check raw sequencing read quality using FastQC.

steps.description: Low-quality regions and adapter fragments were removed from the raw reads based on all known Illumina adapter sequences with the options 2:30:10 via Trimmomatic 0.36 (Bolger et al., 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: Remove low-quality regions and adapter sequences from raw reads using Trimmomatic.

steps.description: The trimmed data were also checked for quality of sequencing before the start of further analysis using fast QC with an average PHRED score of 20.
steps.searchable_summary: Check quality of trimmed reads using FastQC.
outputs: FastQC quality reports for trimmed reads

steps.description: Reads below the length cut-off of 100 nucleotides were discarded.
steps.searchable_summary: Discard trimmed reads shorter than a specified length cutoff (100 nucleotides).

steps.description: Read pairs with only one surviving read were dropped after trimming.
steps.searchable_summary: Drop read pairs where only one read survived the trimming and filtering process.

steps.description: The Q20, Q30, and GC contents were estimated to perform all downstream analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: Estimate quality metrics (Q20, Q30, GC content) for processed reads.

steps.description: Transcriptome assembly was done based on the left. fq and right. fq files using Trinity v2.4.0 (Haas et al., 2013).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform de novo transcriptome assembly using Trinity with processed paired-end reads.

steps.description: The transcript abundance was determined at 0.1 dispersion.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine transcript abundance (likely as part of the assembly or post-assembly process, potentially using a dispersion parameter).
